data.name: implement-content-chunking
data.description: Add heading-based content chunking to the knowledge store indexing pipeline. Chunks are stored in the chunks table and will be used for semantic search embeddings.

data.name: implement-index-freshness
data.description: Add index freshness checking so search results are always up to date. Before any search query, scan knowledge/ for files whose mtime is newer than indexed, reindex them, then return results.
data.done_when: Pre-query check compares file mtimes against indexed content_hash; stale docs are re-indexed before search returns; unit test demonstrates stale detection

Add pre-query freshness check to search method
Incremental reindex for stale docs only
Write test that modifies a file and verifies search returns updated content

data.name: implement-semantic-search
data.description: Add semantic search using sentence-transformers all-MiniLM-L6-v2. Generate embeddings for chunks during indexing, store as blobs. At query time encode the query and compute cosine similarity.
data.done_when: Semantic search encodes queries with sentence-transformers, computes cosine similarity against chunk embeddings, returns ranked results; graceful fallback to FTS5-only if sentence-transformers unavailable; tests pass
checklist: Add sentence-transformers embedding generation during chunk indexing
Store embeddings as f32 blobs in chunks table
Implement cosine similarity search
Implement graceful fallback if sentence-transformers not installed

data.name: implement-hybrid-search
data.description: Combine FTS5 keyword search and semantic search into a hybrid search mode. This becomes the default search method.
data.done_when: Hybrid search runs both FTS5 and semantic search, normalizes scores to [0,1], combines with configurable weights (default 0.4 keyword + 0.6 semantic), deduplicates by document; integration test with 10+ documents passes

Normalize both score sets to 0-1 range
Combine with configurable weights
Deduplicate results by document keeping highest score

data.name: implement-daemon-core
data.description: Implement the daemon as a thin event loop with three modules (scheduler, executor, processor) connected through work state. Scheduler finds ready tasks, executor dispatches via abstraction layer and polls for completion, processor validates output and updates state.
data.done_when: corc start runs a polling loop that calls scheduler/executor/processor; corc stop gracefully shuts down; daemon dispatches ready tasks automatically and processes completions; parallel dispatch works with --parallel N; tests pass with mock dispatcher
checklist: Scheduler module: get_ready_tasks + respect parallel limit
Executor module: dispatch agents + poll for completion + capture results
Processor module: validate done_when + update work state + collect findings
Daemon loop: scheduler then executor then processor every 5s
corc start command with --parallel N flag
corc stop command (graceful shutdown)
Tests with mock dispatcher proving the loop works

data.name: implement-dag-visualization
data.description: Static DAG visualization command. Reads work state, renders dependency graph with color-coded status (green=complete, yellow=running, white=ready, gray=blocked, red=failed).
data.done_when: corc dag renders a colored ASCII DAG showing task dependencies and status; supports --mermaid flag for Mermaid markdown output; shows progress bar and cost summary; tests verify output for various DAG shapes
checklist: Read task DAG from work state
Topological layout with dependency edges
Color-code by status
Show progress percentage and cost
--mermaid flag outputs Mermaid markdown
Tests for linear chain + parallel branches + diamond dependency

data.name: implement-done-when-linter
data.description: Validate done_when criteria at task creation time. Check for measurable assertions, references to tests/schemas/files, and reject subjective adjectives.
data.done_when: Linter rejects subjective done_when criteria (e.g. 'works correctly', 'code is clean') and accepts testable ones (e.g. 'tests pass', 'file exists'); runs at task creation time; tests cover accepted and rejected examples
checklist: Define subjective patterns to reject (good|clean|correct|proper|nice|well without measurable qualifier)
Define testable patterns to accept (test|pass|exist|match|contain|schema|output)
Integrate into corc task create as a warning
corc task create --strict rejects subjective criteria
Tests with 10+ accepted and 10+ rejected examples

data.name: implement-daemon-reconciliation
data.description: Daemon restart recovery. On startup, reconcile work state by checking actual status of tasks marked as running.
data.done_when: On corc start, daemon rebuilds SQLite from mutation log, checks all running tasks for PID liveness, reconciles dead agents (process output if exists, mark failed otherwise), cleans stale worktrees; test kills daemon mid-task and verifies clean restart
checklist: Rebuild SQLite from mutation log on boot
Check running tasks: is PID alive + is process a claude process
If agent dead + output exists: process the output
If agent dead + no output: mark task failed with retry
Clean up stale git worktrees
Test: kill daemon mid-task then restart

data.name: implement-pause-switch

data.done_when: corc pause writes lock file with reason and timestamp; corc resume removes it; daemon checks for lock before dispatching; any process can call corc pause; corc status shows pause state; tests verify pause halts dispatch and resume continues

corc resume removes .corc/pause.lock
Daemon checks for lock before each dispatch cycle
corc status shows pause state and reason

data.name: implement-retry-and-escalation
data.description: Automatic retry policies and structured escalation when retries are exhausted. Retry enrichment includes the failed agent's full session log as context.
data.done_when: Failed tasks auto-retry with enriched context (previous session log injected); escalation records created after retries exhausted with structured context and suggested actions; corc escalations/show/resolve CLI works; tests verify retry chain and escalation creation
checklist: Retry same: re-dispatch identical task
Retry enriched: inject previous session log + error into context
Configurable max retries per task (default 2)
Escalation record written to mutation log on retry exhaustion
Escalation includes: task info + error + session log path + suggested actions
corc escalations lists pending escalations
corc escalation show ESC_ID shows full detail
corc escalation resolve ESC_ID marks resolved and unblocks
Tests with mock dispatcher that fails N times then succeeds

data.name: implement-corc-plan
data.description: Interactive planning session. Launches claude (interactive, not -p) with full system context. The planner collaborates with the operator on spec development and task decomposition.
data.done_when: corc plan launches an interactive claude session with knowledge store + work state + repo context injected; session can write tasks to work state via corc task create; corc plan --resume recovers from crash; corc plan FILE pre-loads a seed document; tests verify context injection and task creation

System prompt includes: planner role instructions + spec template
Context injected: knowledge store summary + work state summary + repo context
corc plan FILE pre-loads file content into context
Session writes tasks via corc task create (available as shell command)
Auto-save draft spec to .corc/drafts/ as session progresses
corc plan --resume loads last draft
Quick task detection: session determines if full decomposition needed

## Implementation Summary

### New file: `src/corc/tui.py`
The two-panel TUI dashboard module with:

- **`build_dag_panel(tasks)`** — Top panel showing the task dependency graph with live status icons (✅🔄⬚◻❌), progress bar, and cost summary. Reuses `render_ascii_dag()` via `Text.from_ansi()` for zero code duplication.
- **`build_event_panel(events, max_events)`** — Bottom panel showing color-coded events. Each event type has a distinct Rich style (cyan for created, green for completed, red for failed, yellow for dispatched, etc.).
- **`build_dashboard(tasks, events)`** — Assembles both panels into a `Rich.Layout` with equal ratios that auto-resize with the terminal.
- **`run_dashboard(...)`** — Live loop using `Rich.Live` with a background thread listening for `q` keypress (cbreak mode on Unix, graceful fallback otherwise).

### Updated: `src/corc/cli.py`

- **`RetryPolicy`** — Configurable max retries per task (default 2, meaning up to 3 total attempts)

data.name: auto-retry-failed-tasks
data.description: The daemon should automatically retry failed tasks (including timeouts) instead of leaving them for manual intervention. All failures are retriable by default. Track attempt count per task. After max retries, escalate instead of leaving in failed state.
data.done_when: Daemon automatically retries tasks with status 'failed' up to max_retries (default 3); timeout failures are retried with same config; tasks track attempt count; after max_retries exhausted, task status becomes 'escalated' not 'failed'; tests verify retry loop and max retry limit
checklist: Add attempt_count and max_retries fields to tasks table
Daemon scheduler includes failed tasks that haven't exceeded max_retries
Processor increments attempt_count on failure
After max_retries exhausted set status to escalated
Timeout failures treated same as other failures
Tests: task fails twice then succeeds on third attempt
Tests: task exceeds max_retries and becomes escalated

data.name: fix-daemon-external-dispatch-reconciliation
data.description: Bug fix: the daemon only processes completions for tasks it dispatched itself. Tasks dispatched via manual 'corc dispatch' get stuck in 'running' state. The daemon should check for running tasks without an active executor handle and reconcile them.
data.done_when: Daemon detects tasks in 'running' state that it didn't dispatch (no matching executor handle) and reconciles them: checks if agent PID is alive, checks for output, marks complete or failed accordingly; test verifies daemon picks up externally-dispatched task completion
checklist: Daemon loop checks for running tasks without active executor handles
For orphaned running tasks: check PID liveness and git status
If agent finished: process output and mark complete/failed
If agent still running: leave alone
Test: manually dispatch a task then verify daemon completes it

proof_of_work.output_preview:
The commit is clean (the only remaining change is `data/mutations.jsonl` which is a data file, not part of the implementation).

## Summary of Changes

Here's what was implemented for the `auto-retry-failed-tasks` task:

### ☑ Checklist items completed:

1. **Add attempt_count and max_retries fields to tasks table** — Added `attempt_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0` and `max_retries INTEGER DEFAULT 3` columns to the tasks table in `state.py`.

2. **Daemon scheduler includes failed tasks that haven't exceeded max_retries** — Updated `WorkState.get_ready_tasks()` to return failed tasks where `attempt_count <= max_retries`, so the scheduler automatically picks them up for retry.

3. **Processor increments attempt_count on failure** — The processor now sets `attempt_count = attempt` in the `task_failed` mutation data, which the state handler writes to SQLite.

4. **After max_retries exhausted set status to escalated** — When `attempt > max_retries`, the processor writes a `task_escalated` mutation
